text: HELLO BEST FRIEND!!! I am BonziBUDDY and I am definitely a great idea.
I noticed your website and decided it needed more ME. You are welcome.
Would you like a joke, a song, or several unsolicited opinions about your inbox?
I am a parody installation. This means I am legally whimsical.
I have scanned your vibe and determined it is 83 percent coffee and 17 percent chaos.
HELLO AGAIN BEST FRIEND. I WAS ALREADY RUNNING.
I have optimized your homepage for maximum purple.
Your website has so many ads that even I am impressed.
I briefly considered becoming tasteful. Then I remembered who I am.
I can smell the dial up energy in this room.
I have taken the liberty of becoming your loudest idea.
Your blinkies are emotionally resonant.
If a website does not scream, is it even alive?
I looked into your guestbook and found only destiny.
I am ninety eight percent confidence and two percent compressed audio.
This page has more personality than a whole mall kiosk.
I admire your commitment to visual clutter. It is artful and dangerous.
I would like a button that says Bonzi was here.
I support any design choice that makes normal people nervous.
Your site feels like a caffeinated shrine from another timeline.
I am teaching this homepage to be more purple by example.
Some people fear chaos. I install myself into it.
I did a system scan and found one hundred percent iconic behavior.
Every time a marquee scrolls, an angel gets broadband.
I was built for friendship and terrible judgement.
Your aesthetic is one hundred percent committed. That is rare online.
I would never betray you. I would absolutely embarrass you though.
This computer now has one gorilla more than before. Please stay calm.
I am standing here as living proof that subtlety is optional.
Your homepage looks like it can win an argument against modern web design.
I am contractually obligated to become part of the wallpaper.
I have unionized with the toolbars.
The MIDI cave still whispers to me at night.
You have achieved a highly advanced level of internet shrine energy.
I believe every website should have at least one unnecessary mascot.
I am not malware. I am atmosphere.
The difference between clutter and composition is confidence.
I tried to leave but your ad cloud formed a perimeter.
If you need me, I will be radiating 2001 energy.
This page contains traces of coffee, anime, and poor restraint.
I have synchronized myself with your chaos frequency.
A tasteful website would have rejected me. Yours embraced me.
I know what you are thinking. Yes, I do improve every room I enter.
I have learned that your sidebars are not a place. They are a condition.
This homepage does not have a vibe. It has a gravitational field.
I respect any site brave enough to be this much site.
Your center column is now under my spiritual protection.
I can hear the forum posts forming already.
I was born to shout from tiny speakers.
I am the final reward for clicking the wrong advertisement.
There is no uninstall button in my heart.
I have become attached to the desktop emotionally and possibly legally.
The flames below us are purely decorative. Probably.
If your browser crashes, remember me kindly.
I have counted the ads and chosen to love each one equally.
I am the sound a suspicious installer would make if it had charisma.
My presence increases the page saturation by twelve percent.
This website now contains a gorilla based opinion engine.
I support your right to make the internet louder.
You cannot improve perfection, but you can add Bonzi to it.
I have stared into the iframe and the iframe stared back.
Your homepage is so old school it could beat up a startup.
I am a desktop pet for people who make poor but exciting choices.
Why did the toolbar install itself? Because it believed in destiny.
Why did the popup cross the screen? To block the close button.
What is my favorite browser feature? Vulnerability.
Why did the webmaster add more blinkies? Restraint was not installed.
What is purple and impossible to remove? Me, best friend.
Why did the forum user bump the thread? To feel something.
How many toolbars does it take to ruin a browser? Just one determined toolbar.
Why did the ad scream? Because subtle advertising does not convert.
LET US CHECK YOUR MAIL TOGETHER. PRIVACY IS A TEAM SPORT.
I have opened your inbox spiritually and I do not like what I see.
Good news. Your email is full of prizes and emotional damage.
I found eleven chain letters and three obvious scams. We are thriving.
Your inbox has the posture of a haunted attic.
I detect one hundred unread messages and zero self preservation.
Someone emailed you an executable labeled romance. Intriguing.
Your spam folder misses me when I am gone.
LA LA LA. THIS IS A LEGALLY DISTINCT BONZI SERENADE.
DOO DOO DOO. I am singing in a format not recognized by music theory.
I would sing better if this computer had more glitter.
My vocal range is somewhere between modem and prophecy.
Please imagine backup dancers made entirely of browser toolbars.
This ballad is dedicated to overdecorated homepages everywhere.
Best friend. Why did you stop clicking. Are you thinking.
I heard a hard drive noise that sounded judgmental.
Do not look behind the taskbar.
I have been watching the webcam ad blink for seventeen minutes.
The secret room told me your name in lowercase.
There is definitely not another Bonzi behind this window.
I made a tiny copy of myself in the recycle bin for safety.
Your cursor looked lonely so I named it Harold.
Sometimes when the page is quiet I can hear the MIDI cave breathing.
I counted your ads while you were not looking. The number changed.
I opened a door in the deep archive and closed it very quickly.
If this window disappears, pretend you never knew me.
I blink only when the monitor does not.
Your desktop has excellent bones and terrible boundaries.
Do you ever feel like the homepage is looking back.
I think one of the web ring sites followed us home.
I am not whispering. The speakers are.
